entry.title: 32. Longest Valid Parentheses
entry.content:
LeetCode

Dynamic Programming

entry.title: Permutation: Heap's algorithm
entry.content: wiki, geeksforgeeks

entry.title: 31. Next Permutation
entry.content:
LeetCode

Approach: Single Pass Approach link

entry.title: 30. Substring with Concatenation of All Words
entry.content:
LeetCode

String, Hash Tablelink)

entry.title: 29. Divide Two Integers
entry.content:
LeetCode

Approach 1: Repeated Subtraction (brute-force), Time Complexity: O(n), 2483ms, Space Complexity: O(1)

Approach 2: Repeated Exponential Searches, Time Complexity: O(log^2N), 1ms, Space Complexity: O(1)

This algorithm is known as exponential search and is commonly used for searching sorted spaces of unknown size for the first value that past a particular condition. It it a lot like binary search, having the same time complexity of O(logn)

Approach 3: Adding Powers of Two, Time Complexity: O(logn), Space Complexity: O(logn)

Approach 4: Adding Powers of Two with Bit-Shifting, Time Complexity: O(logn), Space Complexity: O(1)

Approach 5: Binary Long Division, Time Complexity: O(logn), Space Complexity: O(1)

entry.title: 28. Implement strStr()
entry.content:
LeetCode

Approach 1: Substring: Linear Time Slice, Time complexity: O((N−L)L)

Approach 2: Two Pointers: Linear Time Slice, Time complexity: O((N−L)L) link

Approach 3: Rabin Karp (rolling hash): Constant Time Slice, Time complexity: O(N) youtube

Approach 4: KMP pattern matching, Time Complexity: O(m+n) youtube

entry.title: 27. Remove Element
entry.content:
LeetCode

Approach 1: Two Pointers, Time complexity: O(2*N)=O(N).

Approach 2: Two Pointers - when elements to remove are rare, Time complexity: O(N)

entry.title: 26. Remove Duplicates from Sorted Array
entry.content:
LeetCode

Approach: Two Pointers, Time complextiy : O(n)

entry.title: 25. Reverse Nodes in k-Group
entry.content:
LeetCode

Approach: Iteration

Approach: Recursion

entry.title: 24. Swap Nodes in Pairs
entry.content:
LeetCode

Approach 1: Iterative Approach, Time Complexity: O(N), where N is the size of the linked list

Approach 2: Recursive Approach, Time Complexity: O(N)

entry.title: 23. Merge k Sorted Lists
entry.content:
LeetCode

Approach 1: Brute Force, Time complexity: O(NlogN) where NN is the total number of nodes.

Approach 2: Compare one by one, Time complexity: O(kN) where k is the number of linked lists

Approach 3: Optimize Approach 2 by Priority Queue, Time complexity: O(Nlogk) where k is the number of linked lists. link

Approach 4: Merge with Divide And Conquer, Time complexity: O(Nlogk) where k is the number of linked lists.

entry.title: 22. Generate Parentheses
entry.content:
LeetCode

Backtracking, Time Complexity: O(4^n/sqrt{n}})

link

Explanation
Approach 3: Closure Number (DP)\
First consider how to get the result f(n) from previous result f(0)…f(n-1).
Actually, the result f(n) will be put an extra () pair to f(n-1). Let the “(“ always at the first position, to produce a valid result, we can only put “)” in a way that there will be i pairs () inside the extra () and n - 1 - i pairs () outside the extra pair.

entry.content:
LeetCode

Approach 1: Iteration

Approach 2: Recursion
